steps.title: Understand the goal: emphasize methodology
steps.content:
The student wants to highlight how Kind conducted the study — the actual research process. From the notes, the key action is: Kind and her team reviewed 132 medical school websites, examining student policies. The correct choice must describe this process, not the data location, the results, or the research aim.
  • Choice A — 'The student policies of 132 medical schools can be found online, according to research by Terry Kind.': describes where data is located, not how research was conducted ✗
  • Choice B — 'To find out how many medical schools have guidelines about student social media use, Terry Kind and her team examined the student policies of 132 medical schools.': names the specific action the researchers took — examining policies of 132 schools ✓
  • Choice C — 'Out of 132 medical schools, only thirteen had student policies that mentioned social media, and only five specified what use was acceptable.': reports results, not methodology ✗
  • Choice D — 'Terry Kind and her team wanted to know how many medical schools have student social media policies in place about protecting patient confidentiality.': describes the study's aim, not how it was conducted ✗

steps.title: Confirm the correct choice
steps.content:
Methodology means the steps taken to carry out research. Only choice B describes an action ("examined the student policies of 132 medical schools"), which is precisely the research process from the notes.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
